title: slight difference in error message with inspect between docker and podman
body:
### Feature request description

I'm trying to run some Docker Desktop extensions on top of podman

One of this extension is using the inspect command to figure out if a container is already started or not and search for a string in returned error message. In this case they were expecting `No such object`

like `docker inspect foo` command

in docker we have error message
```
docker inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                                   []
Error: No such object: foo
```

and in podman we have:
```
podman inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                                 []
Error: inspecting object: no such object: "foo"
```

so `No` vs `no` then it was failing the logic




### Suggest potential solution

Of course I've proposed to the extension to search on a lowercase version of the error message

but other extensions could rely on the same kind of logic so I was wondering if Uppercase letter could be added

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context


### 1. diff in `container inspect` as well
```
docker container inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                       []
Error response from daemon: No such container: foo
```
```
podman container inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                         
[]
Error: inspecting object: no such container "foo"
```

### 2. diff in order sequence for network `network {name} not found` vs `network {name}: network not found`
```
docker network inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                         []
Error response from daemon: network foo not found
```

```
 podman network inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                         []
Error: inspecting object: network foo: network not found
```

### 3. for volumes it's OK

```
docker volume inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                            
[]
Error response from daemon: get foo: no such volume
```

```
podman volume inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                            []
Error: inspecting object: no such volume "foo"
```

### 4. diff in default inspect
```
docker inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                                   []
Error: No such object: foo
```

and in podman we have:
```
podman inspect foo                                                                                                                                                                                                 []
Error: inspecting object: no such object: "foo"
```

comments.body: In Podman we enforce no capitalization on error codes, so this would be a huge change. Although I think the `inspecting object` part of this error is just noise and should be removed.

string parsing error messages is always kinda broken. External tools should just check exit codes IMO.
For podman we added `podman container/image/volume/network exists` commands to make this trivial for users but well that doesn't help with docker compat obviously.

yes I know it can be broken, just seeing tools relying on the error message.
I have no opinion. Could be a 'won't fix' I'm just reporting that tools were not working at first when doing the switch from docker engine to podman engine.
Could you open an issue with this plugin, to at least do the string compare after doing a ToLower()
I've already submitted patches (that were merged) to handle the difference in the plugin
Awesome.  I don't see us changing, although I did open a quick PR to remove the extra noise on the error message.

I reopen as I want to explore the issue a bit further.  @benoitf, is this plugin using the CLI or the REST API?  Because we must be compatible on the REST API.

To a certain degree the compatibility includes identical error messages as many tools (including docker-py) check them.

@vrothberg CLI

In fact Docker Desktop extensions have an API to execute commands
https://github.com/microcks/microcks-docker-desktop-extension/blob/main/client/src/api/containers.ts#L35-L36

that is then executed on the host through CLI

And extension can grab error and analyze it
https://github.com/microcks/microcks-docker-desktop-extension/blob/main/client/src/api/containers.ts#L55

(now you can see it's checking using the lowercase)

Thanks for elaborating, @benoitf!

I agree that this will be hard to tackle on the CLI.  We probably need to tackle issues as they come in.
